mnemonics.title: Ethernet Frame Fields
mnemonics.content: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Packet Away (Preamble, Destination MAC, Source MAC, Type/Length, Data, FCS)

mnemonics.title: Ethernet Speed Naming
mnemonics.content: 10-100-1000: BASE means baseband, T means twisted pair, F means fiber, X means encoding scheme

mnemonics.title: CSMA/CD Process
mnemonics.content: Listen Before Talk - Listen While Talk - Jam If Collision - Back Off Random Time

acronyms.expansion: Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection
acronyms.usage: MAC protocol for shared Ethernet media access
Layer 2 addressing scheme for network interfaces
CRC error detection field in Ethernet frames
Marks end of preamble and start of frame

visualTips.visualization: Draw a rectangle divided into: Preamble(7B)|SFD(1B)|DestMAC(6B)|SrcMAC(6B)|Type(2B)|Data(46-1500B)|FCS(4B). Total: 64-1518B
Imagine two people talking simultaneously in a room - they stop, one says 'you go first', then resume after random wait time
XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX - First 3 bytes (OUI) = manufacturer, Last 3 bytes = unique device identifier
Central hub/switch as hub with computers as spokes - like bicycle wheel with switch at center

analogies.analogy: Like a postal sorting office - reads address (MAC) on letter (frame) and sends it only to correct delivery route (port), not to everyone
Like a meeting where people check if someone is speaking before talking, stop if they hear overlap, and wait randomly before trying again
Like a person's unique fingerprint - assigned at birth (manufacture), identifies them uniquely everywhere
Like a physical letter: envelope (header/trailer) has addresses (MAC), stamp (FCS for validation), and contents (data)
